title: Benzoin oxyphyllum
intro: Benzoin oxyphyllum, also known as Styrax tonkinensis, is a plant species belonging to the Styrax family, native to Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Benzoin oxyphyllum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are often used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include benzoin resin, styrax oil, and various terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Benzoin oxyphyllum is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, salves, and essential oils, which are used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Benzoin oxyphyllum is classified as a species of tree in the family Styracaceae. It is native to Asia and is a member of the genus Dryobalanops.
